content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://record.umich.edu/articles/study-examines-pain-vs-gain-of-raising-minimum-wage/", "title": "Study examines pain vs. gain of raising minimum wage", "summary": "Most independent businesses can shoulder the added costs of higher minimum wages with new revenues, making only minor employment adjustments, according to new research from the University of Michigan and Carnegie Mellon University. The study, which draws on the universe of U.S. tax returns to examine the impacts of minimum wages, debunks concerns that small businesses are hit hard by minimum wage increases. Ross School of Business, who co-authored the study with Max Risch from Carnegie Mellon. \u201cOf course, these gains to workers and owners are financed by consumers, who appear fairly inelastic in their overall demand for the goods and services furnished by independent businesses affected by minimum wage policies,\u201d Rao said.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The data compare firms and workers in the states that raised their minimum wages in 2014 to those in states that didn\u2019t.", "Among restaurants, where low-earning workers make up about 16% of variable costs, as opposed to just 6% of production costs among retailers, wage hikes cause some less productive small restaurants to close.", "But they add that, at least when it comes to minimum-wage increases of the size states adopted in 2014, policymakers \u201cneed not worry about a tradeoff between redistribution and job losses or swaths of distressed independent businesses.\u201d"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The data compare firms and workers in the states that raised their minimum wages in 2014 to those in states that didn\u2019t."}, {"url": "https://www.nelp.org/insights-research/minimum-wage-basics-employment-and-business-effects-of-minimum-wage-increases/", "title": "Employment and Business Effects of Minimum Wage Increases", "summary": "Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labor force. This dynamic has spurred the most significant wave of action to raise the minimum wage in fifty years, with momentum for significant increases at the federal, state and local levels. The growing momentum for raising the minimum wage has focused attention on the impact of higher minimum wages on employment levels.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labor force.", "Opponents, by contrast, generally contend that higher wages will reduce employment or slow job growth."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labo"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2211335526000306", "title": "Effects of non-minimum wages on health: A narrative literature review of short-and long-run studies using causal inference or longitudinal data in high-income\u00a0\u2026", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0277953624010803", "title": "The unintended effects of a large minimum wage increase on health: Evidence from South Korea", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/labr.12263", "title": "The effects of minimum wages on (almost) everything? A review of recent evidence on health and related behaviors", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://hcapps.holycross.edu/hcs/RePEc/hcx/LaFiandra_Schwab_MW_safety_10-20-23.pdf", "title": "A higher minimum wage promotes workplace safety: Evidence from the restaurant industry", "summary": "This paper uses a difference-in-differences methodology to exploit staggered changes in the minimum wage from 1992-2016 to demonstrate that an increase in the minimum wage reduces workplace injuries and deaths in the United States restaurant sector, with an elasticity between -0.73 and -1.45. We limit attention to the restaurant sector because minimum wage workers are strongly overrepresented in that sector. Safety violations in routine inspections decline when the minimum wage is increased.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["This paper uses a difference-in-differences methodology to exploit staggered changes in the minimum wage from 1992-2016 to demonstrate that an increase in the minimum wage reduces workplace injuries and deaths in the United States restaurant sector, ", "Most scholars indicate that raising the minimum wage causes a slight decrease in employment, but there are also papers that find no effect or even a positive effect.", "Introduction The American workplace is distressingly dangerous, with 5,333 workplace deaths and 2.8 million nonfatal injuries and illnesses in 2019 (Bureau of Labor Statistics 2019a and 2019b).", "Workplace deaths per 100,000 workers fell steadily from 5.25 in 1992 to a low of 3.25 in 2009 but have edged up slightly since (Bureau of Labor Statistics 2019a).", "Workers earning low wages or the minimum wage are injured at work at a higher rate (Michaels 2015; Leigh, Leigh, and Du 2019), partly because lower wage jobs are more likely to involve physical labor (Bernstein and Gittleman 2003)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: This paper uses a difference-in-differences methodology to exploit staggered changes in the minimum wage from 1992-2016 "}]}
